title: Implement all 8 MCP tool handlers — delegate to existing HTTP endpoints
implementation_notes: Replace the stub handlers from subtask_01 with real fetch() calls.
Each handler pattern (same base URL logic as CLI):
relayhq_list_tasks → fetch GET {BASE_URL}/api/vault/read-model then filter tasks by assignee if provided
relayhq_claim_task → fetch POST {BASE_URL}/api/vault/tasks/{taskId}/claim with { assignee: agentId }
relayhq_heartbeat → fetch POST {BASE_URL}/api/vault/tasks/{taskId}/heartbeat with { assignee: agentId }
relayhq_update_task → fetch PATCH {BASE_URL}/api/vault/tasks/{taskId} with the fields
relayhq_request_approval → fetch POST {BASE_URL}/api/vault/tasks/{taskId}/request-approval with { assignee: agentId, reason }
Error handling: if fetch fails or returns 4xx/5xx, return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Error: {status} {body}' }], isError: true }
For successful responses: return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) }] }
Test manually: run the server, use Claude Code MCP inspector or a test script to call each tool.
acceptance_criteria: All 8 handlers make real HTTP calls to the RelayHQ API.
relayhq_get_context returns workspace summary when server is running.
relayhq_claim_task returns 409 message when task is already claimed.
Error responses include isError: true and a readable error message.
No duplicated business logic — all logic stays in the HTTP handlers.
completion_summary: Implemented all eight MCP handlers as thin HTTP delegates to the RelayHQ APIs with readable error propagation and no duplicated business logic.
